architecture_summary: CMT is a hybrid CNN-transformer architecture for visual recognition that uses a convolutional stem, stage-wise hierarchical feature maps, and CMT blocks combining local perception, lightweight multi-head self-attention, and an inverted residual feed-forward network. It is designed to capture both local and global information while reducing the computational cost of pure transformers.

novel_contributions: a new transformer based hybrid network by taking advantage of transformers to capture long-range dependencies, and of CNNs to extract local information
a family of models, called CMTs
a simple scaling strategy to obtain a family of CMT variants
the introduced CMT block with local perception unit, lightweight multi-head self-attention, and inverted residual feed-forward network

key_claims.claim: CMT-S achieves 83.5% top-1 accuracy on ImageNet with only 4.0B FLOPs, and is 14x and 2x less than the best existing DeiT and EfficientNet, respectively.
CMT-S generalizes well on CIFAR10 (99.2%), CIFAR100 (91.7%), Flowers (98.7%), and COCO (44.3% mAP).
The proposed CMT block helps capture both local and global information and improves efficiency over pure transformers.
CMT-S achieves 83.5% top-1 accuracy with 4.0B FLOPs, 3.7% higher than DeiT-S and 2.0% higher than CPVT.
CMT-S is 0.6% higher than EfficientNet-B4 with less computational cost.
On COCO val2017, CMT-S outperforms Twins-PCPVT-S by 1.3% mAP and Twins-SVT-S by 2.0% mAP in RetinaNet.
On COCO val2017 with Mask R-CNN, CMT-S surpasses Twins-PCPVT-S by 1.7% AP and Twins-SVT-S by 1.9% AP.
